methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Exodys Energy is a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Carl Perez and Jean-Christophe Piroux that has raised $10M+. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $10M as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $25M. A peer-median approach was not feasible due to insufficient data density within narrow peer cohorts at the same stage or similar valuation tier; while 8 nuclear energy peers exist in the dataset, none of the targeted filters produced three or more comparable datapoints, making sector-wide medians unsuitable for precision.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. This range reflects the methodological constraint of relying on a single quantitative approach rather than multiple independent triangulation methods. Key uncertainties include the lack of direct stage-and-sector peer comparables, which limits confidence in the applicability of the 2.5x multiplier to this specific company's profile. Validation against transaction precedents or refined peer cohorts would strengthen conviction in this valuation band.
